title: Assistant Marketing Manager - Travel Retail APAC
description:
Company Overview / Employee Value Proposition

Caudalie is a unique story of love, nature, passion, chance encounters and a sense of adventure! Founded in 1995 by Mathilde and Bertrand Thomas, Caudalie has grown into a leading French natural skincare brand known for its patented grape‑derived active ingredients. Headquartered in Paris and employing between 1,500 people worldwide, the company is committed to creating effective, clean, and sustainable skincare while maintaining strong environmental values, including being a proud “1% for the Planet” member since 2012. We are expanding our teams in our Singapore regional office, join us!

Job Summary

The Assistant Marketing Manager APAC will support driving brand visibility and activation across Greater China and SEAO by coordinating marketing campaigns and operational tasks. This role requires a proactive, organized, and creative professional who thrives in a fast-paced, global environment.

Responsibilities

  • Plan and implement marketing campaigns across APAC Travel Retail to enhance brand visibility and customer engagement
  • Coordinate with sales teams and retailers to ensure timely and effective execution of marketing activations
  • Manage marketing assets including visuals, POS materials, testers, and gifts to ensure compliance with brand guidelines
  • Oversee new store openings in airports by collaborating with key project stakeholders to maintain brand consistency and ensure timely delivery
  • Organize events such as TFWA and Travel Retail seminars in partnership with the sales team to support brand promotion
  • Monitor and analyze marketing campaign performance and ROI to provide actionable insights for continuous improvement
  • Prepare monthly reports on activations to inform stakeholders and guide decision-making
  • Track marketing budgets and expenses by managing quotes, invoices, and validations to ensure financial accuracy
  • Maintain and update marketing tracking files including campaign calendars and POS lists to support operational efficiency

Required competencies and certifications

  • Fluent in English to communicate effectively across regional teams and stakeholders

Preferred competencies and qualifications

  • Proficiency in Chinese to support communication within Greater China markets
  • Strong skills in MS Excel, Word, and PowerPoint to manage data and presentations efficiently
  • Knowledge of digital marketing tools to enhance campaign reach and effectiveness
  • Experience in marketing within the beauty industry to understand sector-specific dynamics
  • Excellent organizational and project management skills to deliver campaigns on time with attention to detail
  • Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced environment and meet deadlines consistently

Headquartered in Paris and employing between 1,500 people worldwide, the company is committed to creating effective, clean, and sustainable skincare while maintaining strong environmental values, including being a proud “1% for the Planet” member since 2012.
